page.title: Authentication
page.lead: Authentication is the process of verifying a client identity before allowing access to protected resources. In backend systems it is essential to control permissions, protect sensitive data, and reduce identity spoofing risk.

importance.title: Why is it essential in backend?
importance.description: In an API, authentication and authorization are not the same. First you validate <mark class="docs-highlight">who you are</mark> (authentication), then you validate <mark class="docs-highlight">what you can do</mark> (authorization). If you skip authentication, any client could perform privileged actions.
importance.note: Minimum best practices: hashed credentials, expiring tokens, session cookies with <mark class="docs-highlight">httpOnly</mark>, and secret rotation.

basic.title: Basic example
basic.description: This example uses an in-memory list to validate username and password. It is ideal to understand the flow before connecting a database.

sessions.title: Session-based authentication
sessions.description: Skyguard provides the <mark class="docs-highlight">sessions()</mark> middleware and three storage strategies: memory, JSON file, and database. All share the same login pattern and access through <mark class="docs-highlight">request.session</mark>.

memory.title: In-memory session storage
memory.description: Recommended for local development or tests. It does not persist if the process restarts.

file.title: File session storage (JSON)
file.description: Use <mark class="docs-highlight">FileSessionStorage</mark> to persist sessions on disk with configurable cookies.

database.title: Database session storage
database.description: With <mark class="docs-highlight">DatabaseSessionStorage</mark> you define an engine-agnostic adapter. This allows direct SQL or ORM with the same contract.
database.note: You can use any database connector or ORM. You only need to implement the adapter class so the framework can recognize your database sessions.
database.configLabel: To keep code cleaner, it is recommended to create a separate file for database session configuration, for example: <mark class="docs-highlight">src/sessions/config.ts</mark>.

jwt.title: Authentication with JSON Web Token (JWT)
jwt.description: Skyguard includes an internal <mark class="docs-highlight">JWT</mark> class to create and verify tokens. The recommended pattern is issuing a token on login and validating it with middleware on protected routes.
jwt.algorithmsWarn: Cryptographic note: <mark class="docs-highlight">HS*</mark> algorithms use a shared symmetric key; <mark class="docs-highlight">RS*</mark> algorithms use an asymmetric key pair (private/public).

rows.recommendedUsage: Internal services with robust secret and rotation
Same use case as HS256 with longer digest
Higher cryptographic margin in strict environments

rows.keyRequirement: Private key to sign and public key to verify
rows.recommendedUsage: APIs with separated issuers and verifiers

rows.keyRequirement: Private key to sign and public key to verify
Private key to sign and public key to verify
rows.recommendedUsage: High security requirements and RSA compatibility
